children.heading: Oversight of Conduct of Independent Counsel.—
children.label: a Oversight of Conduct of Independent Counsel.—

children.heading: .—Congressional oversight
children.label: 1 .—Congressional oversight
children.text: The appropriate committees of the Congress shall have oversight jurisdiction with respect to the official conduct of any independent counsel appointed under this chapter, and such independent counsel shall have the duty to cooperate with the exercise of such oversight jurisdiction.

children.heading: .—Reports to congress
children.label: 2 .—Reports to congress
children.text: An independent counsel appointed under this chapter shall submit to the Congress annually a report on the activities of the independent counsel, including a description of the progress of any investigation or prosecution conducted by the independent counsel. Such report may omit any matter that in the judgment of the independent counsel should be kept confidential, but shall provide information adequate to justify the expenditures that the office of the independent counsel has made.

children.heading: .—Oversight of Conduct of Attorney General
children.label: b .—Oversight of Conduct of Attorney General

children.text: When the information about the case was received.
Whether a preliminary investigation is being conducted, and if so, the date it began.

Whether an application for the appointment of an independent counsel or a notification that further investigation is not warranted has been filed with the division of the court, and if so, the date of such filing.

children.heading: .—Information Relating to Impeachment
children.label: c .—Information Relating to Impeachment
children.text: An independent counsel shall advise the House of Representatives of any substantial and credible information which such independent counsel receives, in carrying out the independent counsel’s responsibilities under this chapter, that may constitute grounds for an impeachment. Nothing in this chapter or shall prevent the Congress or either House thereof from obtaining information in the course of an impeachment proceeding.section 49 of this title
